summary: The unit test suite covers all three retention rules (max_age, keep_latest_n, max_total_size), their union semantics, the DeletionPlan summary, dry-run vs. apply behaviour, error handling, and the CLI end-to-end. Workflow structural validation, actionlint, and act integration tests are also included, giving thorough CI/CD coverage. Rigor is solid but not exhaustive: missing tests for an empty artifact list, size/age exactly at the boundary, keep_latest_n=0, max_total_size_bytes=0, timezone-naive datetime inputs, invalid JSON or missing file passed to the CLI, and the --apply (non-dry-run) CLI path. Design is generally clean — the make_artifact helper and sample_artifacts fixture work well — but the module-level mutable _next_id counter is fragile (test-order-dependent IDs), the module-scoped act_results fixture causes all parametrised act tests to fail en-masse if act itself errors, and test_act_result_file_exists has an implicit ordering dependency on that same fixture. The act integration layer is ambitious and valuable but introduces Docker/network fragility. Overall a trustworthy suite for catching regressions in the happy path and primary edge cases, with some gaps at boundaries and in CLI error paths.
